title: Tactics: Removal of the Guard
summary: Learn to remove the defender and capture protected pieces.
description: The removal of the guard (also called 'defender removal') is a tactical theme where you capture or drive away the piece that defends another piece or critical square. This allows you to capture the defended piece or deliver checkmate. Essential for GM-level tactics.
objectives: Recognize when a piece is protected by another
Understand how to remove the guard
Master combining removal with other tactical themes
plans: Find the piece that is defended - it might be worth capturing
Look for its defender - if you can remove it, you can capture
Often involves a sacrifice to remove the guard
commonMistakes: Don't capture a protected piece without removing the guard first
Remember: sometimes a piece is defended by multiple pieces
The removal can be by capture or by driving the defender away
completionSummary: You've learned to identify and exploit defended pieces through removal of the guard.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Attack f7
steps.coaching: The knight attacks f7, a weak square in Black's position.

steps.title: Black defends f7
steps.instruction: Black pushes d5, attacking the bishop.
steps.coaching: The pawn attacks our bishop on c4. We need to move it.

steps.title: Capture on d5
steps.instruction: Capture on d5 with the pawn!

steps.title: Black pushes
steps.instruction: Black pushes e4, attacking the knight on g5.

steps.title: Capture on f7
steps.instruction: Capture on f7! This attacks the king!
steps.coaching: The bishop captures on f7. But wait - there's a defender!
steps.hint: Capture the knight on f7 with the bishop.

steps.title: King captures
steps.instruction: The king must capture the bishop!
steps.coaching: The king captures on f7. Now look at the position - the knight on g5 is attacking f7, and the queen on e2 is aimed at f7! The bishop was the guard...
